metric_value: Midrange servers exceed IBM ES/9000 in technological capability and systems reliability
notes: I/O bandwidth, processor speed, and systems software all advanced significantly in last 2-3 years

metric_value: 1 to 1,000 active concurrent users; economical from single-employee brokerage to large datacenter
notes: Example: brokerage office in Sri Lanka (1 user) to 1000+ concurrent users

metric_name: Midrange channel I/O bandwidth vs mainframe
metric_value: Midrange: 20-32 MB/sec channel I/O; mainframe ESCOM: 9.5 MB/sec
notes: In 1992 midrange surpassed mainframe in both processor power and I/O bandwidth; high-speed disk arrays also standard on midrange

metric_name: Midrange vs mainframe IS cost reduction
metric_value: 50% average cost reduction in acquisition, maintenance, and support; application development up to 80% less costly
notes: One significant cost increase: network management when migrating to distributed midrange

metric_value: RDBMS is an integrable part of midrange server; provides speed of app dev, portability, customization, departmental customization
notes: Reason for calling them midrange 'servers' not 'computers'

metric_name: Midrange security/data integrity vs mainframe and PC LAN
metric_value: Midrange servers provide greater functionality in security, data integrity, availability than either mainframes or PC LANs
notes: PC LANs have numerous technical deficiencies for OLTP; mainframes inflexible; midrange best of both

metric_value: Falls short of enterprise production requirements in 1993; may change within 2-3 years
notes: Microsoft, Novell, SCO all attempting to create multiuser OS on Intel x86; limited R&D and user knowledge vs dedicated midrange suppliers

metric_value: Midrange servers will move from TCP/IP to OSF DCE within next several years
notes: Aberdeen anticipates continuous change in communications area

metric_value: [UNVERIFIED]

metric_name: HP position in midrange server market
metric_value: Dominant revenue supplier of midrange servers for new enterprise applications
notes: Combined RISC hardware and advanced Unix OS environment software

metric_name: NCR position in midrange server market
metric_value: Market leader for multiuser Intel/Unix midrange servers
notes: Intel 80X86 in innovative multiprocessing architectures running Unix

metric_value: $280 million per year in midrange servers based on SCO Unix
notes: Attacking market from low-end in price and support; moving up value chain

metric_value: Pioneer; standards to which others aspire; but unable to win major new accounts until Alpha/OpenVMS stabilizes
notes: VMS ease of use, DECnet, VAXcluster still best-in-class benchmarks; concentrating on maintaining installed base profitably

metric_value: New generation of transaction monitors for efficiently integrating OLTP apps, heterogeneous databases, and users
notes: Target platform: midrange server OS; Transarc alongside USL Tuxedo and NCR Top End

metric_value: USL (now Novell division) introducing new generation of transaction monitors to tie islands of disparate databases
notes: Critical middleware for integrating diverse applications across large enterprises

metric_value: Three-tier-plus (client + distributed midrange + central production + analytical) is most successful distributed architecture
notes: Based on research with enterprises that successfully implemented production distributed systems

metric_value: Enterprises will implement client-server applications based on RDBMS-of-choice rather than any one manufacturer's midrange platform
notes: Platform-agnostic RDBMS selection becomes the organizing principle

metric_value: [UNVERIFIED]

metric_name: IBM suggestion to emulate Unisys strategy
metric_value: If IBM emulated Unisys A-Series strategy it could extend useful life of ES/9000 mainframes
notes: Aberdeen advisory to IBM leadership

metric_value: Today's midrange servers can be remotely backed up, applications propagated, batch scheduled, and crashes recovered without on-site operators
notes: Major objection from centralized mainframe IS staff overcome; significant cost savings for multi-location enterprises

metric_value: Users connected to midrange server hub have interoperable access to mainframes, PC users, databases worldwide including public data networks
notes: Key long-term benefit; interoperability of many-to-everything

metric_value: Three classes of midrange servers will be chief building blocks: datacenter application servers, gateway servers, distributed production servers
notes: Connected via high-speed fiber optic; gateway servers optimized for high availability and next-gen transaction monitors

metric_value: [UNVERIFIED]
Surround strategy: mainframe as data repository while new OLTP and decision support apps developed on midrange; preserves investment and retrains staff
notes: Allows enterprises to extend operational life of current mainframe databases

metric_value: Advanced servers based upon multiple microprocessor architectures will become standard server platforms of mid-to-late 1990s
notes: Tightly coupled (2-20 CPUs) and loosely coupled (dozens-hundreds) configurations

metric_value: [UNVERIFIED]

metric_name: Unix vs proprietary OS adoption timeline
metric_value: By 1988, most midrange suppliers had to offer Unix-compliant OS to compete; now compete on middleware quality
notes: Driven by government purchasing regulations; middleware (not OS alone) is the real differentiator

metric_name: Distributed systems and network management cost
metric_value: One significant cost component increased by migrating to distributed midrange servers: network management
notes: Caveat to the 50% cost reduction claim

metric_value: Distributed midrange server strategy is best match between technology and enterprise production computing requirements
notes: Wrap-up conclusion; mainframes no longer best solution for enterprise-wide production computing

metric_name: IS decision makers must reassess midrange
metric_value: IS decision makers who evaluated midrange servers even 2 years ago need to reassess functionality advances of 1993 servers
notes: Rate of midrange technology advancement cited as key message
